symbols: gate — threshold between secular and sacred
description: A sepia-toned watercolor sketch showing a stone gate tower with an onion dome topped by a cross, part of a monastery complex. The building has arched doorways and small windows. A long wall extends to the right. A small figure stands near the gate. Cyrillic text below identifies this as the gate of St. Nicholas Church in Gustynia, dated 1845.
theological_description: This watercolor depicts the gate of St. Nicholas Church in the Gustynia Monastery, a historic Orthodox monastic complex in Ukraine. Monastery gates hold deep symbolism in Eastern Christian tradition — they mark the boundary between the secular world and sacred ground, a threshold of spiritual transformation. The onion dome topped with a cross is characteristic of Eastern Orthodox church architecture, pointing heavenward and symbolizing the flame of prayer ascending to God.

scripture_refs.reason: Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with praise
This is none other than the house of God; this is the gate of heaven
